But great to meet you.

I'm based in Houston.
I'm the founding executive here at GrowthX.

I know you were introduced by someone, and I would love to just understand how GrowthX might be relevant to some of the challenges your team's working through.
Yeah, I'll give a quick intro.
I'm Vijay, and then I've been with Pando for a little over two years, and I had the demand generation at Pando.
So Nitin, the CEO, and I were talking to Sandeep.
Sandeep is the one who kind of referred you guys to talk to you folks.
Because one of the things that Pando, what we are doing right now is like, we started entering it in our...
America market probably a couple of years back, and then we scaled up the outbound motion, we scaled up the events portion, but inbound piece is something that we have not scaled it up, right?
And to be fair, we have not invested in inbound also that much for us to expect the results.
So we have not invested, but at the same time, have not seen any results with inbound.
So for us to grow further, for us to kind of scale this business completely, I mean, at the levels that we are expecting, we wanted to crack the inbound engine really well.

And that's the reason why we were talking to Sandeep, and Sandeep kind of suggested, he talked to a couple of folks, and then he kind of suggested your name to talk to you folks, and then understand what you guys are currently doing, and then how you can help us.
So that's how the intro happened, and then what I am trying to solve is like, basically, imagine like, there's a little bit of inbound that's happening at Pando, if you have to set up.
In going from scratch, how do you kind of help us is what I wanted to understand, Tyler.
Perfect.
Super helpful intro.
appreciate all the context.
Tell me a little bit more about content creation, specifically your blog or the editorial parts of your website.
Are you doing that all in-house?
Yeah, that's all.

No, weekly basis, probably a couple of contents.

Okay.
And I guess, do you feel good about where organic traffic is at?

Yeah, yeah.
We are looking to scale that up also.
But I know like with organic traffic, will take a lot of time to kind of drive that organic traffic.
We wanted to scale that up.
but

Yeah, makes perfect sense.
And then I guess like last kind of just broader question here, like, why do you feel like inbound hasn't delivered the results that you're looking for?
Like, what do you feel like the missing pieces are?
No, one of the things is like, we don't have an expertise in-house who can do the inbound for us, who can do a digital marketing for us.
Either we need to hire a resource internally or we need to hire a agency who can help us with that, right?
So we don't have a resource internally who can help us in terms of adverts or like help us in terms of scaling that inorganic search.

That's why we wanted to kind of...
terms of how how we we can can

Well, thanks for all the context.

For the high level, right, like what we're doing here at GrowthX is operating as an end-to-end partner with everything from your GEO and SEO strategy to the content creation to staging and publishing the content in your CMS and delivering the LLM visibility and analytics that you'd be looking for.
to see how performance improves.

about about I I don't don't much

Yeah, you'll take an intro from Sandy pretty seriously, I'm sure.
But the quick and dirty on Marcel is just that he's always in his career seen brands as organizations that should work more like publishers, right?
Like, you can spend all this money on paid, renting your audience, but the path is going to be so much lower if you can build a content engine that over the long term provides value.

So, he was the marketing leader at HashiCorp when they scaled from $6 million to $100 million in two years.

And then at Deepgram, what he did is he took the...

And so that's why we've seen, you know, a lot of the logos that you see here become customers over the last year.

Okay.

A lot of clients are like that.
Number one, you'd be on the low end of the cost, you'd at 20k, because we wouldn't have as much work to do, since you've assembled a lot of that documentation.
Number two, you'll see that we go really deep on the

So fears and uncertainties, motivations, how to win or lose their trust, what they care about, what gets them promoted, what gets them fired.
And so once we've gotten these artifacts in a good place, we'll move to publishing.
Now, that could be week three.
That could be week six.

So, you know, I wouldn't expect, if you're partnered with us, to automatically, in phase one, be seeing, you know, this rapid growth on your organic traffic.
But you should start to see content that matches the bar that you want to hit.
And you should see that the assignments that we're going to create content around in phase two.
This is...
Are...
It's to the opportunities that you see, yeah.
Got it.
This is mostly for the organic traffic, right?
What about the paid ones?
Yeah, so, I mean, on paid, we're not doing anything with our services around paid because our philosophy is that you can do it with content, right?
That content is an asset that's going to pay more dividends than spending in the paid channels.
So it would just be on the content side.
It will be only on the content side, is what you're saying.
Okay, got it.
Exactly.
But the view is, you know, if you can be in these AI answers, you know, do you need to spend on paid, right?
Is that better dollars spent than dollars spent creating content that establishes you as the expert?
Understood, understood.
So, help me understand this, but after the first two weeks, what are the outcome or what are the KPIs that I can measure?
Yeah, would say organic traffic is definitely one that you're going to look at, right?
Your domain authority is going to be another one.
It's just, as you know, like content is more of a long-term investment.
So, you know, with Augment Code, we were able to generate results quickly.
They started working with us in April.
In six months, we've, you know, almost 10X'd their organic traffic.
So that's something that you can look at.
And, you know, if you're in the answers for the topics that your audience cares about, and if the AI search visit traffic is higher intent, then that should, like, lead to more demo requests, right?
So that's sort of why we focus where we do.
Okay, okay.
Got it, got it.
Understood.
Okay.
A good example of just to kind of show you a quick one here, like, we built all these guides for Augment Code.
So it's a lot of, you know, one tool versus another tool in the space.
And as you can continue to continue.
You go down, you know, there's 10 best practices and seven ways, context engineering, you know, supercharges, AI development.
And so it's really like thought leadership and being the answers to a lot of questions in the category that they work in.
And so, you know, for this, we're seeing that, like, articles that we've posted are showing up as the first citation in AI mode, right?
Enterprise AI coding tools, augment code, you know, GitHub, Cursor, there's all these different platforms.

And, you this changes all the time, right?

So we can definitely, you know, get strategic once we've built that strong editorial foundation.
And the idea is to create content relevant to the audience.
It's all audience first.
So this per diem calculator we built for RAMP and created pages for every single city in the state.
This expense classifier is another example of what we created.
So that anytime someone's asking about where an expense should live, know, RAMP is coming up in the answer.
And so that gives you like a look at different types of content we've created for our clients.
Got it.
Understood.
So, yeah.
So, can I, I mean, like the dashboard that.
Everything that you're showing, right?
So I wanted to get a couple of my team members also.

Yeah, absolutely.
I'll go ahead and share this presentation with you.
It goes a lot.
It goes pretty granularly through our process of what we're doing, where we're working, how these different artifacts and personas are defined.
And in terms of what's to measure, this is a good slide that sort of talks about, right, the different things that we would be looking at as we're working with you as a client.
Okay, okay.
If you can just share this with me, I'll kind of take a look at it.
So, what will be a...
Typical ARR, Tyler, I mean, like once the pilot phase is over, what would be the typical 12-month engagement cost?
Yeah, so we would have you at $20,000 for the strategy sprint fixed fee, and then for the phase two growth execution, our clients start at $15,000 per month.
$15,000 per month, okay, okay.
And it's custom, based on customer to customer is what you're saying.
Yeah, in terms of how to think about it, you know, that $15,000 would represent the editorial content, the content refreshes, and we would hit daily publishing with you during that period.
It'd be all the LLM visibility and monitoring.

Got it.

can, it'll be easy for me.

that's the time.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Explore potential partnership between Pando.ai and GrowthX for inbound marketing and content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=7.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Pando.ai seeks to scale inbound marketing, particularly through search and content creation](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=60.0)
  - [GrowthX offers end-to-end AI-powered content strategy and creation, focusing on organic growth](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=249.0)
  - [Engagement model: 8-week strategy sprint ($20k) + 12-month execution phase ($15k/month)](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=607.0)
  - [GrowthX emphasizes LLM visibility and AI-answer optimization for higher intent traffic](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)

## Topics

### Pando.ai's Current Marketing Situation

  - [Successful outbound and event marketing, but underdeveloped inbound strategy](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=60.0)
  - [Creating 1-2 pieces of content weekly, published on blog, LinkedIn, and website](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=147.0)
  - [Lack of in-house expertise for digital marketing and inbound strategies](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0)
  - [Seeking to scale both organic and inorganic (paid) search efforts](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=80.0)

### GrowthX's Offering and Approach

  - [End-to-end partner for GEO/SEO strategy, content creation, and analytics](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=249.0)
  - [Focus on AI-powered content optimization for LLM visibility](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=269.0)
  - [Use of AI workflows with expert oversight for comprehensive, fresh, and well-structured content](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=619.0)
  - [Team includes managing director, managing editor, and content manager with impressive industry experience](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=555.0)
  - [Two-phase engagement: 8-week strategy sprint followed by 12-month growth execution](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=607.0)
  - [Daily publishing during growth execution phase](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=648.0)

### GrowthX's Differentiators

  - [Founded by Marcel, former marketing leader at major tech companies (IBM, HashiCorp, Scale.ai)](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=427.0)
  - [Emphasis on brands operating like publishers for long-term value](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=439.0)
  - [Proprietary AI workflows and upcoming client-facing platform](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
  - [Track record of success with clients like Augment Code and Ramp](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=944.0)

### Content Strategy and Examples

  - [Creation of comprehensive guides, thought leadership pieces, and programmatic content](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=889.0)
  - [Focus on being the first citation in AI-powered search results](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=915.0)
  - [Examples: Augment Code's tool comparison guides, Ramp's vendor database and calculators](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=889.0)

### Measurement and KPIs

  - [Primary focus on organic traffic growth and domain authority](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)
  - [Emphasis on AI search visit quality (3x longer site visits, 4.4x more likely to convert)](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=867.0)
  - [Example: 10x organic traffic growth for Augment Code in 6 months](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=856.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Tyler to share detailed presentation deck with Vijay](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1050.0)
  - [Follow-up meeting scheduled for Wednesday, September 17th at 7:00 AM PST (7:30 PM IST)](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1252.0)
  - [Vijay to review deck and discuss internally with team](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1276.0)
  - [Consider bringing team members to next meeting for deeper platform demo](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1263.0)
  - [Be mindful of 3-5 week lead time for strategy sprint kickoffs if interested in moving forward](https://fathom.video/share/MzQrcN19tdey4B2ScStyX2aqiTyzmJjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1281.0)

action_items.description: Share GrowthX presentation deck with Vijay
Review GrowthX deck, prep questions for next meeting
Discuss GrowthX proposal w/ team before next meeting
Send calendar invite for Wed 7AM PT follow-up meeting w/ Vijay
